hypothesis: Run 10 hit RC_009 TERMINAL_CONSTRAINT (ML literature 2024-2026 density). Run 11 tests if sequential 7-epoch loop on NON-ARXIV tech-leader writing (Paul Graham, Karpathy, Sam Altman, OpenAI blog, Anthropic news, HN, recent ML breakthrough search) can produce paradigm-shift candidates that escape literature-density saturation, with multi-parameter RLHF mimick scoring (10 params) driving weight evolution.
hypothesis_outcome: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. Sequential + multi-parameter scoring produced 24 survivors (composite >= 0.7, Belinda 3Q PASS) across 7 epochs. The escape was enabled by REPLACING Run 10's binary L7 saturation kill with a SOFT 10-parameter composite. Under strict L7 rule, all 24 would still be killed (each has >=1 bridging paper). RC_009 binding is replaced by RC_010 epistemic question: do soft-threshold survivors count as paradigm-shift candidates? Phase 6 cross-LLM queue pending.

design.sequential: ONE source per epoch, in order. R1 enforced.
design.kb_load_per_epoch: Each epoch read persistent_knowledge_base.json + rlhf_weights.json. R2 enforced.
design.kb_update_per_epoch: Each epoch appended findings + updated rlhf_weights. R3 enforced (weight_history 7 entries).
design.multi_param_scoring: 10 parameters per candidate, composite = weighted sum. R4 enforced.
design.counter_paper_grounded_fixes: >=3 web_search per root cause. R5/R6 enforced (32 web_searches total).
design.auto_fetch_no_user_request: WebFetch firewalled, fell back to WebSearch with no user request. R10 enforced.

phases_completed: phase_0: rlhf_weights.json init + epoch_logs/ + 7-epoch directory tree
phase_1: multi_parameter_scorer.py (10-param composite with PARAM_FUNCS table)
phase_2: rlhf_weight_updater.py (gradient on survivor-vs-rejected param means, normalize, snapshot)
phase_3_epoch_6: HN top stories (Penn polariton + Jack Clark RSI + Aalto quantum); 4 survivors
phase_3_epoch_7: WebSearch ML breakthrough (Nested Learning + RLMs + World Models); 6 survivors
phase_6_cross_llm_queue: top-10 by composite queued in phase5_cross_llm/cross_llm_queue.json

kb_delta_from_run_010.rc_009_status_changed: TERMINAL_BINARY -> DESIGN_TRADEOFF (replaced by RC_010 soft scoring)
open_problems_answered: Non-arXiv corpora escape literature-density binding — answered yes WITH soft scoring; no without
open_problems_added: RLHF weight convergence vs oscillation question

rlhf_weight_evolution.interpretation: Cross-disciplinary bridging is the dominant survivor predictor; non-discriminating uniform-1.0 params (belinda/self-publish) properly deweighted; pure-novelty and saturation-distance underperformed cross-source/cross-disciplinary signals.
